skill.name: handoff
skill.description: Creates a concise handoff document so another AI agent can continue the current work.
skill.summary: Summarizes context, next steps, and relevant artifacts for agent handoff.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static external command and filesystem findings are confirmed as real behavior because the skill asks the agent to create a temporary Markdown file. Context shows this is a narrow handoff workflow with no network use, secret access, command construction, or malicious intent. The weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 13 are false positives; no cryptographic algorithm is present.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Handoff Uses mktemp
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to create a handoff document using a temporary file path from mktemp. This is a true positive for external command and filesystem behavior, but the command is fixed and limited to creating a local handoff file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is directly present on line 7. The command text is fixed, and I found no evidence of user-controlled command construction, network access, or secret handling.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Match Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md lines 3 and 13. Those lines contain plain language about creating a handoff document and tailoring it from user arguments, with no cryptographic API, hash algorithm, or encoded payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain no cryptographic terms or executable code. This is a high-confidence false positive based on the actual file content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Fixed External Command Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references mktemp to produce a temporary Markdown file path. This deserves disclosure because it invokes a shell utility, but no injection vector or malicious command is evident.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command reference is explicit and fixed. The risk is low because the skill does not concatenate user input into the command.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local File Write Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to save a handoff document to a temporary local file. This is expected for the skill purpose and does not indicate exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file write instruction is explicit. I found no evidence of writing outside a temporary path or sending the content to an external service.

content.value_statement: Long AI sessions can lose context when work moves to a new agent or chat. This skill creates a focused handoff document with current status, references, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes the active conversation into a handoff document.
Creates a temporary Markdown file for the handoff output.
References existing plans, issues, commits, or artifacts instead of duplicating them.
Suggests useful skills for the next agent session.
Uses optional user arguments to focus the handoff on the next session goal.
limitations: It depends on the current agent having access to the relevant conversation context.
It does not verify whether referenced paths or URLs are complete.
It does not create project plans, PRDs, or ADRs by itself.
It writes a local temporary file and may not be suitable for read-only environments.

use_cases.title: Continue Development Work
use_cases.description: Create a concise handoff before moving an unfinished coding task to a fresh AI session.

use_cases.title: Transfer Research Context
use_cases.description: Capture decisions, open questions, and artifact links before another agent continues investigation.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Long Tasks
use_cases.description: Preserve progress and next actions during multi-step work that spans several assistant sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a new agent needs the current state.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff document for a fresh agent to continue this session.

prompt_templates.title: Focused Next Session
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the next session has a specific goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff focused on implementing the remaining tests and verifying the current changes.

prompt_templates.title: Artifact-Aware Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when important work already exists in files or links.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff that references existing plans, diffs, issues, and notes by path or URL instead of repeating them.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Continuity Handoff
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before a complex project moves to another agent.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a handoff for the next agent with current objective, completed work, important constraints, risks, verification status, and recommended skills.

output_examples.input: Create a handoff for the next session focused on finishing the API tests.
output_examples.output: A Markdown handoff file that lists the current objective, completed changes, remaining API tests, known risks, and referenced project files.

output_examples.input: Prepare a handoff before another agent continues this debugging task.
output_examples.output: A concise transition note with the bug context, attempted fixes, relevant logs, open questions, and the next recommended debugging steps.

output_examples.input: Make a handoff and suggest any skills the next session should use.
output_examples.output: A handoff document that summarizes progress and includes skill recommendations based on the next session focus.

best_practices: Pass a clear next-session goal when you know what the next agent should do.
Keep project artifacts current so the handoff can reference them instead of duplicating content.
Review the generated handoff before ending the session to confirm important constraints are present.
anti_patterns: Do not use it as a replacement for durable project documentation.
Do not include secrets, tokens, or private credentials in the conversation context.
Do not rely on it to recover context that was never available to the current agent.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a Markdown handoff document for another AI agent to continue the current work.

faq.question: Where does the handoff file go?
faq.answer: The skill asks the agent to save it to a temporary file path produced by mktemp.

faq.question: Does it copy existing project documents?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to reference existing artifacts by path or URL instead of duplicating them.

faq.question: Can I guide the handoff focus?
faq.answer: Yes. Pass arguments that describe what the next session should focus on.

faq.question: Does it send data over the network?
faq.answer: No evidence found in SKILL.md that it sends data over the network.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
